presentation_variants.presenting_complaint: Crushing chest pain radiating to the left arm
Severe central chest pressure with sweating
Chest tightness with nausea and arm discomfort
Heavy chest pain spreading to the jaw
Sudden severe chest pain while resting
Pressure-like chest pain with sweating and nausea
Severe chest discomfort radiating into my left shoulder
Crushing chest pain that started suddenly while watching TV

history_data.hpi: Patient experienced sudden onset crushing chest pain while at rest approximately 2 hours ago. Pain is central and radiating to the left arm. Associated with diaphoresis and mild nausea. Denies dyspnea at onset but now feeling uncomfortable.
patient_quotes: It feels like an elephant is sitting on my chest.
I've never had pain this bad before.
The pain keeps spreading into my arm.
I'm starting to feel really worried.
pmh_pool: Family history of coronary artery disease

social_history.smoking: Active smoker, 1.5 packs per day for 30 years

better_with: nothing — pain is persistent and unrelenting

investigations.ECG: ST elevation 2–3mm in V1–V4 with reciprocal ST depression in inferior leads (II, III, aVF). Evolving Q waves in V2–V3. Pattern consistent with acute anterior STEMI — LAD occlusion.
investigations.Troponin: Initial troponin I 3.8 ng/mL (markedly elevated). Repeat at 3 hours: 18.4 ng/mL (rising — confirms ongoing myocardial necrosis).
investigations.Chest X-Ray: No acute cardiopulmonary abnormality on initial film. Normal cardiac silhouette. No pulmonary oedema at time of presentation.
investigations.Echocardiogram: Anterior wall hypokinesis. Moderately impaired LV systolic function, EF 40%. No pericardial effusion. No mechanical complications.
investigations.Urea & Electrolytes: Na 138 mmol/L, K 4.3 mmol/L, Creatinine 96 µmol/L — normal; important baseline prior to contrast for PCI
investigations.Blood Glucose: 11.4 mmol/L (elevated — known diabetes; hyperglycaemia worsens infarct size)
investigations.Lipid Profile: Total cholesterol 6.8 mmol/L, LDL 4.6 mmol/L (elevated), HDL 0.8 mmol/L (low), Triglycerides 2.9 mmol/L (elevated)
investigations.Coagulation Screen (PT/APTT/INR): PT 13s, APTT 27s, INR 1.0 — normal prior to antiplatelet and anticoagulation therapy
investigations.Coronary Angiography: Total occlusion of proximal LAD. Non-obstructive disease in RCA and LCx. Successful primary PCI with drug-eluting stent deployed to proximal LAD. TIMI 3 flow restored.

critical_actions: activate cath lab for primary PCI
establish IV access and cardiac monitoring
red_flags: Radiation to left arm or jaw
management_actions: Activate cath lab — primary PCI target door-to-balloon under 90 minutes
